summary: Comprehensive end-to-end testing of BUMP MVP. 24/24 backend tests PASS (after fixing 1 timezone bug). Frontend mobile flows verified: onboarding renders, login (Ava + admin), signup → profile-setup → home, 8 venues with LIVE badges, active checkin → feed → profile (Like/Hi/Pass), matches tab, settings (edit-profile/hide/leave/sign-out/delete), admin dashboard (overview/users/reports/venues). Demo-mode checkin verified via backend. Fixed timezone-aware datetime comparison bug in /api/matches.

minor.issue: FIXED by tester: mongoDB returns naive UTC datetimes; comparing to offset-aware now() raised TypeError. Added ensure_aware() helper in server.py.

ui_bugs.issue: Admin link not rendered in Settings even when user.is_admin=true. Problem statement requires 'admin link (visible only for admin)'. Admin dashboard is reachable only via direct URL /admin.

Tapping 'Next' CTA does not advance to slide 2 in web preview (FlatList.scrollToIndex on web). Users may be stuck on first slide; final 'Enter the Club' still works because final slide can be reached by horizontal swipe. Consider tracking idx independently or using web-friendly carousel.

issues: Photos for fresh test-registered users render as 400x400 grey placeholder because new users use the small base64 PNG used in test. Real users uploading photos look fine. Seed demo users (Ava, Maya, etc.) are each assigned to distinct venues (i % len(venues)), so Ava is the ONLY seeded demo user at Neon Nights — feed for her venue only contains other test users. Consider seeding multiple demo users at the same venue so demo experience shows other Bump people.

react_native_issues.issue: Console warning: 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents' — appears across screens (likely from LinearGradient/SafeAreaView wrappers). Non-blocking.
Console warning: 'Image: style.resizeMode is deprecated. Please use props.resizeMode' — many occurrences. Non-blocking.

action_items: Add visible 'Admin Dashboard' link/button in Settings screen, gated by user.is_admin (router.push('/admin')).

Investigate onboarding 'Next' CTA: FlatList.scrollToIndex doesn't reliably scroll in expo web; consider using state-driven width offset or react-native-pager-view, or simply track idx and conditionally render slide.
Optional UX: seed more demo users into Neon Nights so Ava's feed has visible matched profiles for demos (currently only 1 seeded user per venue).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:392 mixed naive/aware datetimes from MongoDB — fixed by tester via ensure_aware() helper. Recommend applying same pattern to any other place reading datetime from Mongo (currently only /matches needed it after audit).
Backend file is ~700 lines — within limit but approaching threshold. Future feature additions should split into modules (auth.py, venues.py, matches.py).
Frontend admin gating: check user.is_admin on client side and hide /admin route from nav, but the backend correctly enforces 403 — defense in depth OK.
updated_files: /app/backend/server.py (fixed timezone-aware datetime comparison in list_matches; added ensure_aware helper)

success_rate.frontend: ~90% — all primary flows render and respond; admin link missing from Settings; onboarding Next button web behavior

seed_data_creation: Created several TEST_ users via /api/auth/register during testing (auto-cleaned up by test_delete_account where applicable). Some TEST_Chk users remain in Neon Nights feed from /api/checkin tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend stable at 24/24. To retest: pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v. Frontend admin link & onboarding navigation are the only remaining items.
rca_of_issue: Bug: GET /api/matches returned 500. RCA: MongoDB stores datetime as naive UTC; server.py was creating `now = utcnow()` (offset-aware) and comparing to `m['expires_at']` (naive after Mongo round-trip), raising 'can't compare offset-naive and offset-aware datetimes'. Mitigation: added ensure_aware() to convert all datetimes read from DB to UTC-aware before comparison. Test regression: TestMatches::test_mutual_match_creates now passes.
